title: Lanka — the War of the Ramayana
subtitle: devotion, loyalty and dharma on the causeway to Lanka

raja.name: Rama
raja.moral: Dharma above all — even above the self
raja.teaching: Rama is maryada purushottama, who keeps his word though it costs him a kingdom. Guard him as you would guard your own integrity; when he falls, the cause is lost.

mantri.name: Vibhishana
mantri.teaching: Vibhishana ranges the whole field and dares to leave a wrong side for a right one. Wise counsel that serves dharma is stronger than any army.
mantri.fall: When the true counsellor is lost, kings march blindly into their own ruin.

ratha.name: Ratha
ratha.teaching: The chariot charges the straight road of duty and does not turn aside. Choose your line and press it with force.
ratha.fall: A shattered chariot warns that speed without protection ends in the dust.

gaja.name: Kumbha-Gaja
gaja.teaching: The elephant treads its diagonal with patient, crushing power. Great strength is a blessing only when it keeps to its dharma.
gaja.fall: The great beast falls, and the field learns that pride is heavier than tusks.

ashva.name: Hanuman-Ashva
ashva.teaching: Like Hanuman crossing the ocean, the Ashva leaps where no straight road exists. Faith and courage go where reason alone cannot.
ashva.fall: A fallen leaper reminds us that even the boldest heart is mortal — spend it wisely.

padati.name: Vanara
padati.moral: The strength of the humble many
padati.teaching: The vanaras built a bridge across the sea, stone by stone. The humblest soldier, never retreating, does the patient work that wins the war.
padati.fall: One vanara falls so the bridge may hold — no small sacrifice for dharma is ever wasted.

pawnTeachings: Stone by stone the vanaras bridged the sea — great works are built one small step at a time.
The monkey-warrior never retreats; keep your face toward your dharma.
Even the smallest servant of a great cause carries the whole cause within.
Devotion, not size, moved Hanuman across the ocean — carry your task with a full heart.
Hold the line beside your brothers; a bridge of many hands outlasts any lone hero.
The first stone is the hardest to lay; after it, the causeway is only duty.
You cannot un-lay a stone — weigh each step, then commit it fully.
A humble guard protects more than it strikes; steadfast presence is its own strength.
The sacrifice of the small opens the victory of the whole.
Serve without clinging to reward — build the bridge, and let the sea be crossed.
captureLines: Even in a righteous war, every fallen foe was once someone's kin — strike without hatred.
The victor bows to the fallen; dharma asks for justice, never cruelty.
A piece falls and the causeway advances — attachment is the only true loss.
Do your duty cleanly; the fallen too played their part in the turning of the tale.
Rama mourned even Ravana's rites — honour the enemy you must defeat.
promotionLine: The steadfast Vanara, having crossed the whole field, is raised to a counsellor's rank. Devotion carried to its end becomes wisdom.
checkLine: Rama is threatened — rally the Vanara Sena and shield the heart of the cause.
checkmateTitle: Jaya Shri Rama — Victory of Dharma
checkmateLine: Lanka falls and dharma is restored. Reflect: victory is hollow unless it serves what is right.
